title: NotFullyBroken
description: If elytra don't fully break, why should tools with Mending?
body:
### About
In Minecraft, you can easily break your best tools if something distracts you. **NotFullyBroken** fixes that. Now, all tools with mending and 0 durability will change to their broken state, in which you can't use them. To move them out of the broken state, you simply recover them using XP.

---
### Some Tool Breaking With NotFullyBroken
![NotFullyBroken](https://i.imgur.com/ItFPIV0.gif)
> When tool break, it's moved to the broken state, and back with xp.

---
### Features
- Tools with Mending on brake will change to their broken equivalent, saving all their data.
- Broken tools change to the original item when you absorp xp.
- You can use a broken tool to mine and hit, but it's as efficient as doing this with hand.
- You can't break the broken tool.
- All broken tool assets in 1.21.10
![All Broken Tools in 1.21.10](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/Nln6eVHu/images/73cce033a7db3588c5b6554524d5a24dc6e9c590.png)
